test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
As used in the text, what does the word "follow" most nearly mean?
The following text is from Rudolfo Anaya's 1972 novel *Bless Me, Ultima*. Two of the narrator's adult brothers have decided to move away from home. Another brother has decided not to accompany them.
In the morning León and Eugene were gone, but Andrew remained. They had talked long into the night, and finally he had given up the idea of going with them. I think he did not like to <u>follow</u> their ways, and he wanted to please my mother.
◎1972 by Rudolfo Anaya
question_text: As used in the text, what does the word "follow" most nearly mean?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Justify

choices.label: B
choices.text: Imitate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mention

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restore

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
The Walt Whitman House in Camden, New Jersey, is the house where Walt Whitman lived for eight years and where he wrote later editions of his poetry collection *Leaves of Grass*. Now the house is a museum dedicated to Whitman's life and writings. Visitors travel to this museum to feel more connected to an author they admire. To encourage that sense of connection, the museum's curators have set up exhibits with furnishings and décor owned by Whitman. Such exhibits help visitors understand day-to-day life in the house where Whitman felt inspired.
question_text: Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To describe a museum focused on Whitman's life and works.

choices.label: B
choices.text: To explain why readers enjoy Whitman's works.

choices.label: C
choices.text: To summarize Whitman's biography.

choices.label: D
choices.text: To compare the Walt Whitman House with other museums.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text?
The following text is adapted from Henrik Ibsen's 1899 play *When We Dead Awaken* (translated by William Archer in 1907). Ulfheim is introducing himself to Professor Rubek, a sculptor.
ULFHEIM: I believe <u>I have the honour of addressing no less a person than the great Sculptor Rubek</u>.
PROFESSOR RUBEK: (*Nods.*) I remember meeting you once or twice—the autumn when I was last at home.
ULFHEIM: That's many years ago, now. And then you weren't so illustrious as I hear you've since become.
question_text: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It reveals that Professor Rubek first met Ulfheim many years ago.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It emphasizes that Professor Rubek has a distinguished reputation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It suggests that Ulfheim prefers autumn to spring, summer, or winter.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It indicates that many other sculptors are more famous than Professor Rubek.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely disagree about the answer to which question?
Text 1
Brown University and the investment bank Barclays are two of the many institutions offering training programs in entrepreneurship. But what results do such programs produce? In a study of business school graduates in Norway, researchers Lars Kolvereid and Oystein Moen addressed this question and convincingly demonstrated that participants showed strong intentions to become entrepreneurs as a result of receiving entrepreneurial training.
Text 2
While studies of entrepreneurial training typically report positive results, a close look at these studies reveals widespread methodological shortcomings. For instance, a 1997 study by Lars Kolvereid and Oystein Moen found benefits of entrepreneurial education for graduates of Norwegian business schools, but the study only collected data on subjects after the training, not before, making it unclear whether the training actually had an effect.
question_text: Based on the texts, the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 would most likely disagree about the answer to which question?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Is the cost of conducting entrepreneurial training programs typically offset by the programs' benefits?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Did Kolvereid and Moen's study provide persuasive evidence that its results are attributable to the entrepreneurial training participants received?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Did Kolvereid and Moen report that participants who received the entrepreneurial training showed strong intentions to become entrepreneurs?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Are there convincing experimental results showing that different types of entrepreneurial training programs are associated with different outcomes?

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Information in the text best supports which statement about Zhao et al.'s findings?
Scientists study the abundance and the activity ratios (AR)—the relative radioactive decay rates of two isotopes—of uranium (U) isotopes in bodies of water to track sediment transport and deposition and to monitor groundwater quality. However, isotope abundance and AR are not always uniform across a single body of water, which may affect the reliability of results if sampling is not sufficiently representative. Chen Zhao et al. analyzed concentrations of the isotope U-238 and the U-234/U-238 AR at multiple depths and locations in two lakes in China, finding no significant variation in values within the same lake.
question_text: Information in the text best supports which statement about Zhao et al.'s findings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They illustrate why scientists investigating sediment transport and deposition and groundwater quality ought to evaluate how evenly U abundance and AR are distributed in the bodies of water they are examining.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They challenge the assumption that U abundance and AR sometimes vary from lake to lake, since the U-238 concentration and U-234/U-238 AR were consistent across the sampled lakes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They indicate that researchers performing isotopic analysis in the lakes Zhao et al. studied can likely rely on localized sampling within each lake, since values from any one location or depth are representative of the entire lake.

choices.label: D
choices.text: They demonstrate that the number of samples Zhao et al. collected from the two lakes is sufficient to yield equally reliable information about sediment transport and deposition and groundwater quality in those two lakes.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
One challenge faced by researchers studying global urbanization is that different countries have different definitions of what constitutes an urban settlement. Many countries define urban settlements based on the number of people living in them. However, countries do not all use the same number; for example, Greenland uses a minimum population of 200, while Benin uses 10,000. Other countries use a combination of population and other factors, like how close buildings are to each other. This variation makes it difficult for researchers to compare aspects of urbanization in different countries.
question_text: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is difficult for researchers to compare aspects of urbanization globally because of the various ways different countries define urban settlements.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Even though countries use different methods to define urban settlements, researchers consider any area having a population of at least 10,000 to be urban.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Researchers focus on factors other than population when conducting studies on urbanization in Greenland.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Urbanization is easily compared between countries when they have similar total populations.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which potential finding from another study, if true, would most directly strengthen the team's conclusion?
Psychologists Gregory Bryant, Dorsa Amir, and colleagues investigated cross-cultural perceptions of spontaneous (real) laughter and volitional (fake or forced) laughter. Study participants from 21 societies, including those in Japan and Turkey, listened to randomized recordings of 18 spontaneous laughs taken from natural conversations between pairs of women and 18 volitional laughs produced separately by 18 different women in response to an instruction to laugh. Analysis of the participants' evaluations of the laughs prompted the team to conclude that the ability to distinguish between spontaneous and volitional laughter appears to be universal across cultures.
question_text: Which potential finding from another study, if true, would most directly strengthen the team's conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Listeners from both Japan and Turkey tend to be able to determine how well two people know each other based on recordings of their conversation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Spontaneous laughter typically has a recognizably higher pitch than volitional laughter, regardless of what society the speakers in a conversation are from.

choices.label: C
choices.text: There is some evidence of correlation between the average size of communities in a society and judgments of spontaneous and volitional laughter by listeners in that society.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Acoustic features of spontaneous laughter, such as pitch and intensity, have measurable variations both within and across societies.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which additional finding from Yuan and colleagues' study, if true, would best support the researchers' conclusion?
Certain species, such as Hay's Spring amphipods (*Stygobromus hayi*), lack functional eyes or ocular organs altogether and rely on other mechanisms for navigation. Licheng Yuan and colleagues investigated such mechanisms in the Chinese red-headed centipede (*Scolopendra subspinipes mutilans*), which has no organs with photoreceptors (light-detecting cells) but can sense and avoid sunlight. The researchers placed individual specimens in a clear container (container 1) connected to a container covered in black tape (container 2) and then exposed the containers to simulated sunlight. They observed a rapid increase in the temperature of the centipedes' antennae with the onset of exposure, followed by the centipedes' escape to container 2. Based on their observations, the researchers concluded that thermal receptors in the antennae are likely responsible for light detection in the centipedes.
question_text: Which additional finding from Yuan and colleagues' study, if true, would best support the researchers' conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When body segments other than the centipedes' antennae were monitored, the temperature of these body segments were found to increase in the presence of sunlight.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The centipedes showed the same tendency to move to container 2 when simulated sunlight was accompanied by artificially reduced temperatures.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Chinese red-headed centipedes are more proficient at detecting light than other centipede species are.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When their antennae were covered with a reflective material, the centipedes remained exposed in container 1 for longer durations.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Introducing a new species into a delicate ecosystem can often be harmful. The common waxbill and other fruit-eating birds have established themselves on the Hawaiian island of Oahu after being brought there in the last 150 years. Many plant species on the island rely on the activity of fruit-eating birds to maintain and increase their populations. In recent studies, common waxbills have been found to spread seeds of native forest plants that are at risk of extinction, such as the olomea shrub and the alahee tree, suggesting that ______
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: non-native birds, such as the common waxbill, are unlikely to eat fruits from non-native forest plants on Oahu.

choices.label: B
choices.text: non-native birds have had both positive and negative effects on Oahu's native plants.

choices.label: C
choices.text: although non-native birds initially damaged the plants in Oahu's ecosystem, they are now proving useful to them.

choices.label: D
choices.text: rather than harming Oahu's ecosystem, some non-native birds provide an important benefit on Oahu.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
Which choice most logically completes the text?
Many Indigenous songs serve as repositories of ecological knowledge—e.g., Yi songs about the natural environment and O'odham songs about desert plants. In a study by ethnobiologist Dana Lepofsky et al., a song keeper for the Kwakwaka'wakw people provided access to ancient songs referring to the people's creation of terraced intertidal gardens to foster clams for consumption. Cross-references of that information with both archaeological and paleoecological records have revealed significant increases in clam size and productivity concurrent with the most prevalent use of these clam gardens. This finding suggests that researchers should consider ______.
question_text: Which choice most logically completes the text?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the strong implication that the beneficial effects are primarily attributable to ecological factors unrelated to the activities of the Kwakwaka'wakw people

choices.label: B
choices.text: the possibility that other songs of other Indigenous peoples transmit ecological information that has useful practical applications

choices.label: C
choices.text: the reliability of archaeological and paleoecological evidence that often underpins conclusions drawn from Indigenous songs

choices.label: D
choices.text: whether the Kwakwaka'wakw people originated the method of cultivation detailed in the songs or adapted methods observed among other groups of Indigenous peoples

passage:
The student wants to emphasize the number of SNOTEL sites in Wyoming. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Snow hydrologist Zoe Wellschlager helps maintain automated snow measurement stations throughout the western United States.
• They are called SNOTEL sites.
• There are around 90 sites throughout Wyoming.
• There is one at Cottonwood Creek.
question_text: The student wants to emphasize the number of SNOTEL sites in Wyoming. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In Wyoming alone, there are around 90 SNOTEL sites located throughout the state.

choices.label: B
choices.text: One particular SNOTEL site is located at Cottonwood Creek in Wyoming.

choices.label: C
choices.text: SNOTEL sites collect snow measurement data throughout the state of Wyoming.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Snow hydrologist Zoe Wellschlager helps maintain SNOTEL sites, such as the one at Cottonwood Creek, Wyoming.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
The student wants to emphasize the location of Hornsdale Wind Farm. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Wind is a renewable energy source.
• A wind farm uses turbines to convert wind into electrical power.
• A wind farm's maximum electrical output is called its nameplate capacity.
• Hornsdale Wind Farm is located in Hornsdale, Australia.
• Its nameplate capacity is 316 megawatts.
question_text: The student wants to emphasize the location of Hornsdale Wind Farm. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Hornsdale Wind Farm has a nameplate capacity of 316 megawatts.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Nameplate capacity is another name for a wind farm's maximum electrical output.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hornsdale, Australia, is home to Hornsdale Wind Farm.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Turbines can be used to generate electricity from a renewable energy source: the wind.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
The student wants to compare the disadvantages of the geothermal systems used at the Wayang Windu and Raft River plants. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• The Wayang Windu geothermal power plant in Indonesia uses a flash steam system to generate electricity.
• Flash steam technology requires geothermal reservoir temperatures above 180℃.
• The use of flash steam systems worldwide is limited because many geothermal reservoirs aren't hot enough.
• The Raft River geothermal power plant in Idaho uses a binary cycle system.
• Binary cycle technology can generate energy from lower-temperature (less than 180℃) geothermal reservoirs.
• Binary cycle systems involve higher maintenance costs than flash steam systems.
question_text: The student wants to compare the disadvantages of the geothermal systems used at the Wayang Windu and Raft River plants. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Compared with the system used at the Wayang Windu plant, the system used at the Raft River plant has a notable disadvantage: its cost.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Unlike the Raft River plant, the Wayang Windu plant uses a flash steam system, which requires a less common resource: geothermal reservoirs with temperatures above 180℃.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The system used at the Raft River plant overcomes the temperature limitations of the Wayang Windu plant's system but is more costly to maintain.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The system at the Wayang Windu plant requires temperatures above 180℃, while the system at the Raft River plant can operate at lower temperatures.

test_name: October 2025 US Form A SAT English Module 2
passage:
The student wants to emphasize a similarity between *ArcelorMittal Orbit* and *Čumil the Sewer Worker*. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• *ArcelorMittal Orbit* is an artwork by Sir Anish Kapoor and Cecil Balmond.
• It is a 375-foot steel tower with a spiraling staircase and working slide.
• It is on display in a public park in London, England.
• *Čumil the Sewer Worker* is a work of art by Viktor Hulík.
• It is a bronze sculpture of a man emerging from under a manhole cover.
• It is on display on a public street in Bratislava, Slovakia.
question_text: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between *ArcelorMittal Orbit* and *Čumil the Sewer Worker*. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: *ArcelorMittal Orbit* and *Čumil the Sewer Worker* are both on display at public locations.

choices.label: B
choices.text: *ArcelorMittal Orbit*, but not *Čumil the Sewer Worker*, can be found in London, England.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both *ArcelorMittal Orbit* and *Čumil the Sewer Worker* were created by Sir Anish Kapoor and Cecil Balmond.

choices.label: D
choices.text: *Čumil the Sewer Worker*, a bronze sculpture of a man emerging from under a manhole cover, is on display on a public street.
